```json
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The abstract's central claim invokes 'lightweight instruction tuning' of a 'frozen CLIP-based VLM' — these are contradictory (frozen weights cannot be instruction-tuned); the contribution claim requires reconstruction to be internally coherent.",
    "R1.2": "Three load-bearing logical failures: (1) calibration under distribution shift is asserted but no out-of-distribution evaluation is reported; (2) the Mann-Whitney separation statistic measures discrimination, not calibration — the two are conflated throughout; (3) 'matching or exceeding softmax baselines' appears in the abstract but no baseline comparison appears in the results.",
    "R1.3": "The central evidence-claim gap is severe: no ECE, Brier score, or reliability diagram is reported, yet 'well calibrated' is the load-bearing claim. The qualitative failure-case inspection (n=10) is presented as supporting the confidence-hedging story with no quantitative backing.",
    "R1.6": "Improving the draft naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level quality; several constructions are imprecise ('We interpret this gap as evidence that…' asserts too strongly given only observational data)."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I revise or weaken substantive claims that have no reported supporting evidence — specifically the distribution-shift calibration claim and the softmax-baseline comparison — or should I restrict improvements to writing clarity and flow while leaving all empirical claims intact?"
}
```

rationale.R1.1: The abstract's central claim invokes 'lightweight instruction tuning' of a 'frozen CLIP-based VLM' — these are contradictory (frozen weights cannot be instruction-tuned); the contribution claim requires reconstruction to be internally coherent.
rationale.R1.2: Three load-bearing logical failures: (1) calibration under distribution shift is asserted but no out-of-distribution evaluation is reported; (2) the Mann-Whitney separation statistic measures discrimination, not calibration — the two are conflated throughout; (3) 'matching or exceeding softmax baselines' appears in the abstract but no baseline comparison appears in the results.
rationale.R1.3: The central evidence-claim gap is severe: no ECE, Brier score, or reliability diagram is reported, yet 'well calibrated' is the load-bearing claim. The qualitative failure-case inspection (n=10) is presented as supporting the confidence-hedging story with no quantitative backing.
rationale.R1.6: Improving the draft naturally includes sentence- and paragraph-level quality; several constructions are imprecise ('We interpret this gap as evidence that…' asserts too strongly given only observational data).

parsed.clarification_question: Should I revise or weaken substantive claims that have no reported supporting evidence — specifically the distribution-shift calibration claim and the softmax-baseline comparison — or should I restrict improvements to writing clarity and flow while leaving all empirical claims intact?
